#redirect[[en:Kawashimo Shrine (Hakodate, Nezaki Town)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''川濯神社'''（かわすそじんじゃ）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]][[根崎町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。
== 概要 == [[根崎宮の川]]の東岸[[海岸段丘]]上にある。[[1664年]]（[[寛文]]4年）7月、現在地に[[分霊|勧請]]・鎮祭された。[[1903年]]（[[明治]]36年）に[[神社建築|社殿]]を改築。その後一時期なかったようで、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）に別の場所にて再建され、[[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年)に現在地に移転している。[[伝承]]によればかつては近隣の[[漁師]]が[[境内]]の[[木|樹木]]を目印に[[漁]]にでていたという。社殿は[[権現造]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁 2023年10月21日閲覧</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫命]]<ref name="jinjyacho"/>
== 行事 == [[祭|祭礼]]の執行は、町会長、奉讃会部長（[[氏神|氏子]]代表であり[[1983年]]<昭和58年>）以後は[[推薦|推薦制]]）を中心に行われるが、春祈祷、夏祈祷（大祓い）は[[亀田八幡宮]]の[[宮司]]によって行われる。また[[破魔矢]]など[[縁起物]]は亀田八幡宮から取り寄せる<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302"/>。 * 初詣 - [[大晦日]]から[[元日|元旦]]にかけて、氏子代表を中心に六、七人が徹夜でこもる。 * 春神楽 - 2月20日頃 * 春祈祷 - 4月20日頃 * 夏祈祷（大祓い） - 6月20日頃 * 夏神楽 - 8月15日から16日
== 関連項目 == * [[亀田八幡宮]] * [[湯川町]] - 地域が元々下湯川村だったが、[[1883年]]（明治16年）分村「根崎村」、[[1902年]]（明治35年）[[銭亀沢村]]へ編入。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998年
== 外部リンク == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 川濯神社] - [[神社庁|北海道神社庁]]による案内

== 関連項目 == * [[亀田八幡宮]] * [[湯川町]] - 地域が元々下湯川村だったが、[[1883年]]（明治16年）分村「根崎村」、[[1902年]]（明治35年）[[銭亀沢村]]へ編入。

[[1975年]]（昭和50年）8月29日、[[函館工業高等専門学校]]渋谷道夫助教授によって[[江戸時代]]前期の修験僧（廻国僧）[[円空]]が彫った'''円空仏'''が発見された<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>。

== 関連項目 == * [[亀田八幡宮]] * [[湯川町]] - 地域が元々下湯川村だったが、[[1883年]]（明治16年）分村「根崎村」、[[1902年]]（明治35年）[[銭亀沢村]]へ編入。

{{デフォルトソート:かわすそしんしや}} [[Category:函館市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]]

{{画像提供依頼|社殿|date=2023年10月}} {{神社 | 名称 = 川濯神社 | 画像 = | 所在地 = [[北海道]][[函館市]]根崎町558番地 | 緯度度 = | 緯度分 = | 緯度秒 = | 経度度 = | 経度分 = | 経度秒 = | 祭神 = 木花開耶姫命 | 神体 = | 社格 = 村社 | 創建 = [[1664年]]（[[寛文]]4年）7月 | 本殿 = 権現造 | 別名 = 根崎川濯神社 | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 8月15日・16日 | 神事 = 松前神楽 | 地図 = }}
'''川濯神社'''（かわすそじんじゃ）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]根崎町にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。
== 概要 == [[根崎宮の川]]の東岸[[海岸段丘]]上にある。[[1664年]]（[[寛文]]4年）7月、現在地に[[分霊|勧請]]・鎮祭された。[[1903年]]（[[明治]]36年）に[[神社建築|社殿]]を改築。その後一時期なかったようで、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）に別の場所にて再建され、[[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年)に現在地に移転している。伝承によればかつては近隣の漁師が境内の樹木を目印に漁にでていたという。社殿は[[権現造]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁 2023年10月21日閲覧</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>。
[[1975年]]（昭和50年）8月29日、[[函館工業高等専門学校]]渋谷道夫助教授によって[[江戸時代]]前期の修験僧（廻国僧）[[円空]]が彫った'''円空仏'''が発見された<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫命]]<ref name="jinjyacho"/>
== 行事 == [[祭|祭礼]]の執行は、町会長、奉讃会部長（[[氏神|氏子]]代表であり[[1983年]]<昭和58年>以後は推薦制）を中心に行われるが、春祈祷、夏祈祷（大祓い）は[[亀田八幡宮]]の[[宮司]]によって行われる。また[[破魔矢]]など[[縁起物]]は亀田八幡宮から取り寄せる<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302"/>。 * 初詣 - [[大晦日]]から[[元日|元旦]]にかけて、氏子代表を中心に六、七人が徹夜でこもる。 * 春神楽 - 2月20日頃 * 春祈祷 - 4月20日頃 * 夏祈祷（大祓い） - 6月20日頃 * 夏神楽（例大祭） - 8月15日から16日<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== 交通 == * 根崎神社前停留所 - [[函館バス]][[下海岸線]]（91、91A、91C系統）
== 関連項目 == * [[亀田八幡宮]] * [[湯川町]] - 地域が元々下湯川村だったが、[[1883年]]（明治16年）分村「根崎村」、[[1902年]]（明治35年）[[銭亀沢村]]へ編入。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980年 * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998年
== 外部リンク == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 川濯神社] - [[神社庁|北海道神社庁]]による案内
{{デフォルトソート:かわすそしんしや}} [[Category:函館市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kawasuso Shrine | Image = {{画像募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Founded = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate Names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enku Buddha|ja|円空仏}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enku|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (Annual Festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enku Buddha|ja|円空仏}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enku|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (Annual Festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (Annual Festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (Annual Festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

{{Shrine | Name = Kawasuso Shrine | image = {{画像募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecrated = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate Names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{画像募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>

== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980年 * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998年

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980年 * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998年

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980年 * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998年

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4qq</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buqqha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4qq</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In aqqition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4qq</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In aqqition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4qq</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In aqqition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In a11ition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition[[Category:addition]], lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980年 * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998年

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition[[Category:addition]], lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980年 * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998年

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition[[Category:addition]], lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
