{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year }} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year }} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903rd year }} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year }} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition[[Category:addition]], lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

In addition[[Category:addition]], lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year }} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | Alternate names = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year }} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903rd year }} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937th year }} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967th year }} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975th year }} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year }} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition[[Category:addition]], lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975th year }} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year }} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition[[Category:addition]], lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year }} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903rd year }} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937th year }} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967th year }} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975th year }} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year }} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition[[Category:addition]], lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year }} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903rd year }} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937th year }} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967th year }} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975th year }} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year }} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮}}. In addition[[Category:addition]], lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980th year * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998th year

== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=draft}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year|12=draft}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=draft}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢|12=draft}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日|12=draft}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=draft}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日|12=draft}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|12=draft}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|12=draft}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=draft}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|12=draft}} | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|12=draft}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|12=draft}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=draft}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|12=draft}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903rd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築|12=draft}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937th year|12=draft}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967th year|12=draft}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|12=draft}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975th year|12=draft}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=draft}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空|12=draft}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=draft}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year|12=draft}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=draft}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢|12=draft}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日|12=draft}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=draft}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日|12=draft}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|12=draft}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|12=draft}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=draft}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|12=draft}} | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|12=draft}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|12=draft}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=draft}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|12=draft}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903rd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築|12=draft}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937th year|12=draft}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967th year|12=draft}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|12=draft}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975th year|12=draft}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=draft}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空|12=draft}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=draft}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year|12=draft}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=draft}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢|12=draft}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日|12=draft}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=draft}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日|12=draft}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|12=draft}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|12=draft}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|12=draft}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|12=draft}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|12=draft}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980th year * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998th year

Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} classification is that of a village shrine.

On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975th year|12=draft}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=draft}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空|12=draft}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>

== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=draft}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year|12=draft}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=draft}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢|12=draft}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日|12=draft}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=draft}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日|12=draft}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|12=draft}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|12=draft}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|12=draft}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|12=draft}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:川濯神社 (函館市根崎町) up to rev 102191740
revision.text:
{{画像提供依頼|社殿|date=2023年10月|cat=函館市}} {{神社 | 名称 = 川濯神社 | 画像 = | 所在地 = [[北海道]][[函館市]]根崎町558番地 | 緯度度 = 41 | 緯度分 = 46 | 緯度秒 = 8.4 | 経度度 = 140 | 経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 38.1 | 祭神 = 木花開耶姫命 | 神体 = | 社格 = 村社 | 創建 = [[1664年]]（[[寛文]]4年）7月 | 本殿 = 権現造 | 別名 = 根崎川濯神社 | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 8月15日・16日 | 神事 = 松前神楽 | 地図 = }}
'''川濯神社'''（かわすそじんじゃ）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]根崎町にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。
== 概要 == [[根崎宮の川]]の東岸[[海岸段丘]]上にある。[[1664年]]（[[寛文]]4年）7月、現在地に[[分霊|勧請]]・鎮祭された。[[1903年]]（[[明治]]36年）に[[神社建築|社殿]]を改築。その後一時期なかったようで、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）に別の場所にて再建され、[[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年)に現在地に移転している。伝承によればかつては近隣の漁師が境内の樹木を目印に漁にでていたという。社殿は[[権現造]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁 2023年10月21日閲覧</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>。
[[1975年]]（昭和50年）8月29日、[[函館工業高等専門学校]]渋谷道夫助教授によって[[江戸時代]]前期の修験僧（廻国僧）[[円空]]が彫った'''円空仏'''が発見された<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p411</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫命]]<ref name="jinjyacho"/>
== 行事 == [[祭|祭礼]]の執行は、町会長、奉讃会部長（[[氏神|氏子]]代表であり[[1983年]]<昭和58年>以後は推薦制）を中心に行われるが、春祈祷、夏祈祷（大祓い）は[[亀田八幡宮]]の[[宮司]]によって行われる。また[[破魔矢]]など[[縁起物]]は[[亀田八幡宮]]から取り寄せる<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302"/>。 * 初詣 - [[大晦日]]から[[元日|元旦]]にかけて、氏子代表を中心に六、七人が徹夜でこもる。 * 春神楽 - 2月20日頃 * 春祈祷 - 4月20日頃 * 夏祈祷（大祓い） - 6月20日頃 * 夏神楽（例大祭） - 8月15日から16日<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== 交通 == * 根崎神社前停留所 - [[函館バス]][[下海岸線]]（91、91A、91C系統）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980年 * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998年
== 外部リンク == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 川濯神社] - [[神社庁|北海道神社庁]]による案内
{{デフォルトソート:かわすそしんしや}} [[Category:函館市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]]

== 祭神 == * [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫命]]<ref name="jinjyacho"/>
== 行事 == [[祭|祭礼]]の執行は、町会長、奉讃会部長（[[氏神|氏子]]代表であり[[1983年]]<昭和58年>以後は推薦制）を中心に行われるが、春祈祷、夏祈祷（大祓い）は[[亀田八幡宮]]の[[宮司]]によって行われる。また[[破魔矢]]など[[縁起物]]は[[亀田八幡宮]]から取り寄せる<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302"/>。 * 初詣 - [[大晦日]]から[[元日|元旦]]にかけて、氏子代表を中心に六、七人が徹夜でこもる。 * 春神楽 - 2月20日頃 * 春祈祷 - 4月20日頃 * 夏祈祷（大祓い） - 6月20日頃 * 夏神楽（例大祭） - 8月15日から16日<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== 交通 == * 根崎神社前停留所 - [[函館バス]][[下海岸線]]（91、91A、91C系統）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980年 * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998年
== 外部リンク == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 川濯神社] - [[神社庁|北海道神社庁]]による案内
{{デフォルトソート:かわすそしんしや}} [[Category:函館市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980th year * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998th year

== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|qq=}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|qq=}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1664|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1903|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine architecture|qq=}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1937|qq=}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1967|qq=}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-zukuri|qq=}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 29|qq=}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|qq=}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|qq=}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1983|qq=}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|qq=}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hamaya|qq=}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Eve|qq=}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 20|qq=}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 20|qq=}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 20|qq=}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15|qq=}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 16|qq=}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|qq=}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|qq=}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|qq=}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1983|qq=}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|qq=}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hamaya|qq=}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Eve|qq=}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 20|qq=}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 20|qq=}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 20|qq=}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15|qq=}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 16|qq=}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|qq=}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|qq=}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|qq=}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate City|qq=}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|ja|1664年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1664|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|lt=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|lt=Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-zukuri|qq=}} | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15|qq=}}–{{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 16|qq=}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakodate City|ja|函館市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}. Its former {{ill|typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/typeing System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|qq=}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|qq=}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|ja|1664年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1664|qq=}} ({{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 4). In {{ill|1903|ja|1903年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1903|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 36), the {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine architecture|qq=}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|ja|1937年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1937|qq=}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|ja|1967年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1967|qq=}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|lt=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|lt=Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-zukuri|qq=}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|August 29|ja|1975年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 29|qq=}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|qq=}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|qq=}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=}}). Since {{ill|1983|ja|1983年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1983|qq=}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|qq=}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|hamaya|ja|破魔矢|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hamaya|qq=}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|大晦日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Eve|qq=}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|ja|2月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 20|qq=}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|ja|4月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 20|qq=}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|ja|6月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 20|qq=}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|ja|8月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15|qq=}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 16|qq=}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|qq=}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|qq=}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4). In [[:en:1903|1903]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 36), the [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]] was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in [[:en:1967|1967]] (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|qq=}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=}}). Since [[:en:1983|1983]] (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|qq=}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | consecration_year = [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4) July | Main Hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[:en:August 15|August 15]]–[[:en:August 16|August 16]] | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|qq=}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|qq=}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4). In [[:en:1903|1903]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 36), the [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]] was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in [[:en:1967|1967]] (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|qq=}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=}}). Since [[:en:1983|1983]] (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|qq=}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|qq=}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|qq=}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|qq=}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980th year * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998th year

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | consecration_year = [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4) July | Main Hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[:en:August 15|August 15]]–[[:en:August 16|August 16]] | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|qq=}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|qq=}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4). In [[:en:1903|1903]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 36), the [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]] was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in [[:en:1967|1967]] (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|qq=}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=}}). Since [[:en:1983|1983]] (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|qq=}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|qq=}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|qq=}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|qq=}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980th year * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998th year

{{Shrine | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4) July | Main Hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[:en:August 15|August 15]]–[[:en:August 16|August 16]] | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4). In [[:en:1903|1903]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 36), the [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]] was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in [[:en:1967|1967]] (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Since [[:en:1983|1983]] (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|qq=}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|qq=}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|qq=}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980th year * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998th year
== External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ Kawasuso Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Agency

Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|qq=|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|qq=|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|qq=|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:川濯神社 (函館市根崎町)]] [[ja:川濯神社 (函館市根崎町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q123407867]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4) July | Main Hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[:en:August 15|August 15]]–[[:en:August 16|August 16]] | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|qq=|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}}, [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4). In [[:en:1903|1903]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 36), the [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]] was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in [[:en:1967|1967]] (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}). Since [[:en:1983|1983]] (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|qq=|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}}, [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4). In [[:en:1903|1903]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 36), the [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]] was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in [[:en:1967|1967]] (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}). Since [[:en:1983|1983]] (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}}, [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4). In [[:en:1903|1903]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 36), the [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]] was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in [[:en:1967|1967]] (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}). Since [[:en:1983|1983]] (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 函館市史 通説編第1巻 函館市 1980th year * 函館市史 銭亀沢編 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 1998th year
