<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:川濯神社 (函館市根崎町)]] [[ja:川濯神社 (函館市根崎町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q123407867]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4) July | Main Hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = [[:en:August 15|August 15]]–[[:en:August 16|August 16]] | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}}, [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4). In [[:en:1903|1903]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 36), the [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]] was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in [[:en:1967|1967]] (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}). Since [[:en:1983|1983]] (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}}, [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4). In [[:en:1903|1903]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 36), the [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]] was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in [[:en:1967|1967]] (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}). Since [[:en:1983|1983]] (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}). Since [[:en:1983|1983]] (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as [[:en:Hamaya|hamaya]] are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from [[:en:Ōmisoka|New Year's Eve]] to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around [[:en:February 20|February 20]] * Spring Purification: Around [[:en:April 20|April 20]] * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around [[:en:June 20|June 20]] * Summer Kagura (festival): From [[:en:August 15|August 15]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}}, [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in [[:en:1664|1664]] ([[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 4). In [[:en:1903|1903]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 36), the [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]] was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in [[:en:1967|1967]] (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On [[:en:1975|August 29]] (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk [[:en:Enkū|Enkū]] was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>

Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|en|1664|WD=Q7581|lt=1664}} ({{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri}} | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15}}–{{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}}, {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|WD=Q26418|lt=Hakodate City}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}. Its former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|en|1664|WD=Q7581|lt=1664}} ({{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 4). In {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 36), the {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=shrine architecture}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=1937}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|1975|en|1975|WD=Q2479|lt=August 29}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|en|Enkū|WD=Q2406940|lt=Enkū}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}). Since {{ill|1983|en|1983|WD=Q2434|lt=1983}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|Hamaya|en|Hamaya|WD=Q138804|lt=hamaya}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|Ōmisoka|en|Ōmisoka|WD=Q2357104|lt=New Year's Eve}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|en|February 20|WD=Q2344|lt=February 20}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=June 20}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15}} to {{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

Since {{ill|1983|en|1983|WD=Q2434|lt=1983}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|Hamaya|en|Hamaya|WD=Q138804|lt=hamaya}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|Ōmisoka|en|Ōmisoka|WD=Q2357104|lt=New Year's Eve}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|en|February 20|WD=Q2344|lt=February 20}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=June 20}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15}} to {{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:川濯神社 (函館市根崎町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:川濯神社 (函館市根崎町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:川濯神社 (函館市根崎町)]] [[ja:川濯神社 (函館市根崎町)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|WD=Q26418|lt=Hakodate City}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|en|1664|WD=Q7581|lt=1664}} ({{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri}} | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15}}–{{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Nesaki Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}}, {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|WD=Q26418|lt=Hakodate City}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}. Its former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|en|1664|WD=Q7581|lt=1664}} ({{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 4). In {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 36), the {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=shrine architecture}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=1937}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|1975|en|1975|WD=Q2479|lt=August 29}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|en|Enkū|WD=Q2406940|lt=Enkū}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}). Since {{ill|1983|en|1983|WD=Q2434|lt=1983}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|Hamaya|en|Hamaya|WD=Q138804|lt=hamaya}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|Ōmisoka|en|Ōmisoka|WD=Q2357104|lt=New Year's Eve}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|en|February 20|WD=Q2344|lt=February 20}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=June 20}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15}} to {{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|WD=Q26418|lt=Hakodate City|1=Hakodate}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Q124496601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|en|1664|WD=Q7581|lt=1664|1=1664}} ({{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15|1=August 15}}–{{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16|1=August 16}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Q124496601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496601}}, {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|WD=Q26418|lt=Hakodate City|1=Hakodate}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}. Its former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|en|1664|WD=Q7581|lt=1664|1=1664}} ({{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4). In {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 36), the {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=shrine architecture|1=Shinto architecture}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967|1=1967}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|1975|en|1975|WD=Q2479|lt=August 29|1=1975}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|en|Enkū|WD=Q2406940|lt=Enkū|1=Enkū}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ujigami|WD=Q767655}}). Since {{ill|1983|en|1983|WD=Q2434|lt=1983|1=1983}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|Hamaya|en|Hamaya|WD=Q138804|lt=hamaya|1=Hamaya}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|Ōmisoka|en|Ōmisoka|WD=Q2357104|lt=New Year's Eve|1=Ōmisoka}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|en|February 20|WD=Q2344|lt=February 20|1=February 20}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=June 20|1=June 20}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15|1=August 15}} to {{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16|1=August 16}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|Hamaya|en|Hamaya|WD=Q138804|lt=hamaya|1=Hamaya}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|Ōmisoka|en|Ōmisoka|WD=Q2357104|lt=New Year's Eve|1=Ōmisoka}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|en|February 20|WD=Q2344|lt=February 20|1=February 20}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=June 20|1=June 20}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15|1=August 15}} to {{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16|1=August 16}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>

After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967|1=1967}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|1975|en|1975|WD=Q2479|lt=August 29|1=1975}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|en|Enkū|WD=Q2406940|lt=Enkū|1=Enkū}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}<ref name="jinjyacho" />
== Rituals and Festivals == Shrine festivals are carried out under the leadership of the town council president and the head of the Hōsan Association (representing the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ujigami|WD=Q767655}}). Since {{ill|1983|en|1983|WD=Q2434|lt=1983|1=1983}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|Hamaya|en|Hamaya|WD=Q138804|lt=hamaya|1=Hamaya}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|Ōmisoka|en|Ōmisoka|WD=Q2357104|lt=New Year's Eve|1=Ōmisoka}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|en|February 20|WD=Q2344|lt=February 20|1=February 20}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|WD=Q2666|lt=June 20|1=June 20}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|WD=Q2774|lt=August 15|1=August 15}} to {{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16|1=August 16}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja|根崎神社前停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Shrine Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Served by the {{ill|Hakodate Bus|ja|函館バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Bus|1=Hakodate Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395940}} {{ill|Kaigan Line|ja|下海岸線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan Line|1=Shimokaigan Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123407559}} (91, 91A, 91C routes)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kawasuso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Hakodate City}} | location = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|qid=Q26418|lt=Hakodate City|1=Hakodate}}, {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Q124496601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124496601}} 558 | Latitude = 41 | Latitude minutes = 46 | Latitude seconds = 8.4 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 38.1 | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | Sacred Object = | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | consecration_year = {{ill|1664|en|1664|qid=Q7581|lt=1664|1=1664}} ({{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4) July | Main Hall = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} | alternate_name = Nesaki Kawasuso Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|qid=Q2774|lt=August 15|1=August 15}}–{{ill|August 16|en|August 16|qid=Q2812|lt=August 16|1=August 16}} | Rituals = Matsumae Kagura | Map = }}
'''Kawasuso Shrine''' (川濯神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nesaki Town|ja|根崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Town|1=Q124496601|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124496601}}, {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|qid=Q26418|lt=Hakodate City|1=Hakodate}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}. Its former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification is that of a village shrine.
== Overview == The shrine stands on a {{ill|marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Marine terrace|qid=Q3518589}} on the east bank of the {{ill|Nesaki Miya River|ja|根崎宮の川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaki Miya River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established and enshrined at its current location in {{ill|1664|en|1664|qid=Q7581|lt=1664|1=1664}} ({{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4). In {{ill|1903|en|1903|qid=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 36), the {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|qid=Q3080012|lt=shrine architecture|1=Shinto architecture}} was renovated. After a period without a building, it was reconstructed in {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Showa 12) at another location and moved to its current site in {{ill|1967|en|1967|qid=Q2648|lt=1967|1=1967}} (Showa 42). According to tradition, fishermen in the area once used the trees within the shrine precincts as landmarks for fishing. The main hall is built in the {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} style.<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p377-378</ref><ref name="jinjyacho">[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E5%B7%9D%E6%BF%AF%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-2/ 『川濯神社』] 北海道神社庁, accessed October 21, 2023</ref><ref name="zenigamezawahen_302">函館市史 銭亀沢編 p302</ref>
On {{ill|1975|en|1975|qid=Q2479|lt=August 29|1=1975}} (Showa 50), an {{ill|Enkū|ja|円空仏|q=Enkū|lt=Enku Buddha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enkū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 円空|1=Enkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2406940}} carved by the Edo period itinerant monk {{ill|Enkū|en|Enkū|qid=Q2406940|lt=Enkū|1=Enkū}} was discovered by Shibuya Michio, an associate professor at the Hakodate National College of Technology.<ref>函館市史 通説編第1巻 p4dd</ref>

Since {{ill|1983|en|1983|qid=Q2434|lt=1983|1=1983}} (Showa 58), these positions have been filled by recommendation. Seasonal prayers, such as the Spring and Summer Purifications (Oharai), are performed by priests from the {{ill|Kameda Hachimangu|ja|亀田八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameda Hachimangu|1=Kameda Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22118378}}. In addition, lucky charms such as {{ill|Hamaya|en|Hamaya|qid=Q138804|lt=hamaya|1=Hamaya}} are supplied by Kameda Hachimangu.<ref name="zenigamezawahen_302" /> * New Year's Visit (Hatsumōde): Ujiko representatives keep watch through the night from {{ill|Ōmisoka|en|Ōmisoka|qid=Q2357104|lt=New Year's Eve|1=Ōmisoka}} to {{ill|New Year's Day|ja|元旦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Day|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 元日|1=New Year's Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q196627}}. * Spring Kagura: Around {{ill|February 20|en|February 20|qid=Q2344|lt=February 20|1=February 20}} * Spring Purification: Around {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|qid=Q2524|lt=April 20|1=April 20}} * Summer Purification (Oharai): Around {{ill|June 20|en|June 20|qid=Q2666|lt=June 20|1=June 20}} * Summer Kagura (festival): From {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|qid=Q2774|lt=August 15|1=August 15}} to {{ill|August 16|en|August 16|qid=Q2812|lt=August 16|1=August 16}}<ref>函館市史 銭亀沢編 p406-408</ref>
